exercises.description: Order the sentences (B-G) below to make a story. {v} The first sentence of the story (A) is given for you as an example. {v} A Roald Dahl was a British novelist and is considered one of the best writers in this field.
exercises.label: Reading Part 2

questions.title: B The first children’s story that he wrote was The Gremlins.
C He was born on 13th September 1916, in South Wales, to Norwegian parents.
D Charlie and the Chocolate Factory was his second novel, inspired by Roald Dahl’s love for chocolate.
E The story tells the adventures of a young child inside a chocolate factory.
F He died in Oxford, England, in the year 1990.
G As a child, he loved taking photographs and carried a camera around.

exercises.description: The sentences below are from some instructions, Put these sentences in the right order. The first sentences are done for you {v} A. If you are going to use your new phone, you could follow these instructions.

questions.title: B You should fill up your information and link it up with your email address.
C Open the box of your new phone and let it charge for two hours.
D Next enjoy and take care of your new phone.
E You can then turn on the phone and connect it to a Wi-Fi network.
F After that download the apps that you want and personalize the phone.

exercises.heading: Traveling
exercises.description: Person A {v} I myself am a bit of a travel addict and have visited many countries in my life and used many different forms of transportation. The article talks about how air travel is the safest way of travel, but when it comes down to it, I really feel a lot more safe when taking the train or going by car. The only negative I can think of for road travel would be that petrol prices might be high and overnight parking can sometimes be difficult to find in new cities. {v} Person B {v} Travelling by plane is just too much work in my opinion. Between arriving at the airport early, going through check-in, security, remembering my passport and boarding pass, not to mention the length of the journey, it’s all just extremely complicated. But whenever I do travel, my focus is always on arriving at my destination and exchanging currency - it’s fascinating to hold foreign bills in my hand! {v} Person C {v} A few years back I decided to ship my bicycle over to another country and pick it up in a rental car so I could drive up into the mountains and do some cycling. Everything went smoothly getting my bicycle registered as special baggage, but when I tried to rent the car, they told me that my drivers license wasn’t valid in that country. When I finally got to my hotel by taxi I found out that my bike had a flat tyre so I had to cancel the whole plan. {v} Person D {v} There’s a lot of discussion on the different forms of transport available to us nowadays. However, as a budget traveler I must say that the bus is my go-to option. Yes, it may take a bit longer but it costs me less money and gets me to my destination without fail. My second choice would be the train, although I find the platforms to be disorienting and I always fear that I’m going to miss it.
exercises.subtitle: Four people respond to an online magazine about different tips on travelling abroad. Read their comments and answer the questions below.
exercises.label: Reading Part 4

questions.title: 1. Who has had trouble traveling by car?
2. Who thinks land travel is better than air travel?
3. Who thinks that traveling by car is expensive?
4. Who doesn’t like long trips?
5. Who likes to use cheaper forms of transportation?
6. Who thinks land travel can be confusing?
7. Who wasn’t able to complete their trip successfully?

exercises.description: Read the text below. Match the headings A-H to the paragraphs 19-25.
exercises.label: Reading Part 5

answers: C. The concept of sustainable consumer behaviour
A. What’s to be expected if we don’t change our ways
questions.title: 19.Sustainable consumer behaviour refers to the behaviour of consumers that has social, as {v} well as environmental benefits, along with meeting their needs. Concerns regarding {v} the conservation and sustainability of natural resources have led to the encouragement {v} of consumers to adopt a green way of life. Companies associated with the production {v} of goods and services invest a lot of time, effort and money to study consumer {v} behaviour as a whole. This is because a product or a service impacts all the stages of the {v} process of consumption. It is popularly believed that sustainable development is the {v} answer for the survival of the future generations. The first step towards sustainability is {v} its incorporation into the consumption behaviour of the customers.

answers: E. Limiting consumption for sustainability purposes
questions.title: 20.  The only way to protect the only habitable planet, Earth, and secure the continuance of {v}  future generations is to make sustainable consumption choices. Over-consumption is {v} considered one of the major causes of environmental degradation. Sustainable {v} consumption is all about doing a lot more with less. Implementing the three R’s - reduce, {v} reuse and recycle - in everyday life is a wonderful way to prevent the loss of more {v} natural resources. Learning to life a minimalistic lifestyle, cutting out the unnecessary {v} excess from your life, is a way to help out both your bank account and the environment.

answers: B. Making a difference in daily living
questions.title: 21. Other practical steps you can take towards making a difference include organising anti-{v} littering campaigns and encouraging people in your community to plant trees. You can {v} also contribute towards sustainable development by embracing new habits, such as {v} turning off the light when leaving the house, or taking shorter showers to save water. {v} Using CFL or LED lightbulbs, rather than traditional ones, also makes a difference in how {v} much waste you produce, because they last longer and are more energy efficient.

22. Alternative forms of energy for electricity, such as hydropower or solar energy, is another {v} effective way to prevent the extinction of fossil fuels like, coal, oil, and natural gas, {v} thereby protecting the Earth’s limited resources. Also, it will help in reducing global {v} warming caused by too much carbon dioxide in the atmosphere. Changes must be made {v} in the way we view and use different methods of transportation. Use of cars and planes, {v} for example, have a much greater impact on greenhouse gas production than bicycles, {v} trains, or simply just walking. Even taking the bus, though it still contributes to carbon {v} dioxide production, is a better option than driving your own car.

23. Food and drink choices also directly impact the global warming crisis. A lot of people {v} wrongly believe that sustainable eating means avoiding meat or any and all animal {v} products. However, that is not the case. When trying to choose sustainable options, all {v} you have to keep in mind is to eat lower than the food chain. You can eat locally grown {v} and produced fruits and vegetables in order to eliminate the food miles. Also, {v} remember to buy fresh so that you can avoid the processing steps that involve water and {v} carbon footprint. Even the most popular drinks, such as tea and coffee, are big {v} contributors to your carbon footprint, whilst freshly-made fruit juices, milk, and water are {v} better alternatives.

answers: G. Steps being taken in the housing industry
questions.title: 24. Sustainable homes refer to houses that are constructed using a combination of natural {v} resources and recycled materials. Their aim is to reduce the adverse environmental {v} impact both before and after the construction of a home. This goes a long way in ensuring {v} that the needs of the present generation are met without compromising the future {v} generation's ability to meet their needs. Many home developers are also investing in {v} brand new construction techniques that promote the recycling of the home. These homes {v} are being called “demountable houses.” They can be entirely taken apart and re-{v} assembled again. Energy-efficient homes are also amongst the top preferred properties {v} in the real estate market.

answers: F. Who must participate in sustainable living?
questions.title: 25. The role of humans in promoting and maintaining sustainability is crucial. Individuals can {v} spread awareness about global warming and the dangers of consumerism. It is {v} humans themselves who can protect their planet by working together to find new {v} technologies and alternatives to limited natural resources. This is a responsibility that’s {v} not limited to a select few, but must be shared by humans across the world if we want {v} to see real change in the health of this planet both today, and for future generations to {v} come!

questions.title: Question 1: What is his opinion on the future of artificial intelligence? {x}
answers: He thinks that artificial intelligence is great but it will never replace the work a human can do.
He thinks that the advances of artificial intelligence will only increase over time.
He thinks that artificial intelligence will be the most useful in the field of medicine.

questions.title: Question 2: What is his opinion on the advantages of artificial intelligence compared to humans? {x}
answers: He thinks that with artificial intelligence tasks can be completed more quickly and more accurately.
He thinks that humans will always have more knowledge about a task than any type of artificial intelligence.
He doesn’t think that human emotions are a relevant point when comparing humans to artificial intelligence.

answers: The rich, because it allows them to grow richer.
The customers, because they are able to find a larger range of products.

title: Make Your Mark
description: Describe what you see, express what you think, and connect with others — creativity is your common language here.
